observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to look into the disposal and segregation of wastes in the MRF. The office should supervise and consider the conduct of trainings/workshops on the proper handling and segregation of wastes to personnel of Solid Waste Management. Henceforth, comply with all applicable rules and regulations of RA 9003 to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We further recommended that the LGU’s dumpsite which appears like an open dumpsite be converted into at least controlled dumpsite in the meantime that the LGU has no sanitary landfill facility.
observations.managementAction:
The MENRO is committed to ensuring compliance with Republic Act No. 9003 (Ecological Waste Management Act of 2000) and to safeguarding public health and the environment.
(a). Supervision of Waste Disposal and Segregation – we will initiate a thorough review of the current practices and ensure that they align with the provisions of RA 9003.
(b). Conduct of Trainings and Workshops – we will conduct comprehensive training sessions and workshops on proper waste handling and segregation and on health and safety protocols.
(c). Conversion of Open Dumpsite to Controlled Dumpsite – the following measures are undertaken; site assessment and planning, implementation of control measures & monitoring and maintenance. Construction of the central material recovery (MRF) is ongoing, this will play a crucial role in enhancing our waste management capabilities by; (1). waste segregation and processing, (2) resource recovery & (3). educational outreach.
observations.reason: Both audit recommendations were partially still ongoing due to: 1). Outdated waste infrastructure hindered full compliance, especially if upgrades or new facilities are needed but not yet available. 2). Limited public awareness of proper waste management practices and health risk associated. 3). Weak enforcement of RA 9003 due to limited personnel and lack of trained staff and expertise on waste management. 4). Limited funds to fully transitions to compliant waste management systems such as sanitary landfill.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant in ensuring that the withheld contributions and loans of the officers and employees were properly recorded and remitted in full and intact within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
LDRRM Council to expedite the implementation of all identified PPAs in the LDRRMFIP. Moreover, coordinate with other implementing office to reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise, reprogram the same to other priority disaster-related PPAs in order to attain the objectives of the Fund.
LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to follow the prescribed format provided in Annex A and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the preparation of the LDRRMFIP and Report on Utilization of the LDRRMF. Likewise, prepare and submit the utilization report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

In view of the above, we reiterate our recommendation that the LGU re-evaluate and re-assess the technical viability of proposed projects before inclusion in the list of priority projects under the 20% DF in line with the requirement that the identified PPAs should be well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready. protection of public health and environment.

open dumpsite be converted into at least controlled dumpsite in the meantime
that the LGU has no sanitary landfill facility.

plan to optimize the available fund intended for disaster risk mitigation.

Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73;

remaining 1 technical staff of the LDRRMO in compliance with RA 10121

Engineer to submit explanation for the time extensions and work suspensions
granted to contractors and the pertinent documents relative thereto for proper
evaluation such as the Letter Request by the contractors, PAG-ASA daily

projects awarded to the same contractor to avoid unjustified delays.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the monthly and year-end Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the
LGU’s bank accounts and to record all valid reconciling items prior to the
preparation of the year-end FS so that the Cash in Bank-LCCA account is
fairly presented.

2.10 We recommended that the municipal treasurer and the municipal assessor
prepare data on real property through a duly certified list showing the name
of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index
card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the municipal accountant for
the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the actual Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables of the municipality.

3.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare reconciliation between the two accounts and prepare the
necessary adjusting entry to ensure that movements made on the receivable
account in the transferring fund have corresponding movements on the payable
account in the receiving fund.

4.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to trace accounting entries affecting the Due to BIR, Due to Pag-
IBIG and Due to GOCCs, including prior years’ accounting entries, to
identify the causes of the negative balances and effect necessary adjustments
thereto.

5.6 We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to prepare adjusting
journal entry to transfer the amount of ₱2,204,189.57 from the General Fund
to the Trust Fund to correct the balance of Trust Liability-LDRRM account.
Henceforth, transfer the yearly unutilized balance of LDRRM funds at the
end of the year to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liability account at
every accounting period.

6.9 We recommended that the LCE:
c) Require the Property Officer/GSO and the Municipal Accountant to
maintain stock cards and property ledger cards, respectively;
d) Require the Property Officer/GSO to properly assign unique Property
Number to each item of PPE for easy reference;
e) Initiate the formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of
the PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are
verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

7.9 We recommended that the LCE require:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that warranties and retention
money are required from the contract awardees in their procurement of
goods and services, pursuant to RA 9184 and its IRR;
b. The Municipal Accountant and other officials/employees concerned to
ensure the completeness of data/information and other supporting
documents on the procurement and distribution/consumption of goods
and services.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the
required warranty securities.
8.8 Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects,
particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

9.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to install measures that will facilitate the
submission of the Contracts and their supporting documents within the
prescribed period to facilitate legal, auditorial review, technical evaluation
and inspection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

10.14 We recommended that management:
a. Fast track the implementation of the PPAs consistent with the MOAs for
the benefit of the targeted constituents; and
b. Return to the Source Agency the balances of funds for completed
projects, as well as the unutilized funds for projects which could not be
implemented, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 to enable the
National Government to re-appropriate the funds to other priority
programs.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be
required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers complete
with relevant supporting documents, and to keenly observe the deadline set on
the submission of paid disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents,
monthly trial balances and other financial reports. We also recommend that
the Municipal Treasurer provide measures for the immediate release of checks
to their respective payees in order to avoid this cause of the delay in the
submission of accounts to the auditor.

12.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant and
Treasurer to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents. Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with
the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of
the RRSA.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension/Disallowance as a result of our audit.

13.8 We recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Treasurer to strictly
require all the Revenue Collectors to turn-over daily and intact, all
collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-
382. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the Report of
Collections submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned
over to her, sign the certification and receipt portion and deposit intact all
collections.

14.4 We recommended that the LCE Require the LGU Officials concerned:
a. To fast track the implementation of the Capital Outlay PPAs of the
DRRM funds;
b. Henceforth, implement all LDRMM –PPAS as programmed in the AIP
and LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to enhance the LGU’s pre-
disaster preparedness and post-disaster response and recovery capability
in times of calamities.

15.9 We recommended that management implement immediately the PPAs
funded under the 20% DF of the current and prior years. Henceforth
implement within the timeline as scheduled in the AIP, thru an effetive
monitoring on the part of the MPDC, consistent with the mandated
responsibility of other LGU officials, as embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG
Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the appropriate municipal officials to initiate the processing of the TCT for all acquired real properties, in the name of the LGU under the Torrens Title System, so that the LGU shall have clean title and the assets are safeguarded from possible third party claims.  The needed documents should be compiled for each property and be submitted as soon as possible to the appropriate government offices, for the needed transfer of title.

We recommend further that the MCPC be pro-active in carrying out the relevant programs, projects and activities as envisioned in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120, implement other pertinent national and local laws in order to attain optimum benefits for the children specially the delinquents and disadvantaged.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory, determine the actual condition of these assets and render a report thereon pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.
observations.reason: No inventory committee was created and no inventory report was prepared and submitted for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare JEV to reclassify the assets from Land to Road Networks account
Prepare Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each road and its components.

We recommended that management:
initiate the process and procedures on the write-off of the pertinent receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 particularly those aged 10 years or more, and,
provide Allowance for Impairment for the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Trust Liability accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to trace the history and present the complete records and information relative to the above items to prove its validity and existence and make the appropriate adjusting journal entries if necessary.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entry to rectify the improper presentation of the of the non-current portion of the loans Payable account of P1,554,472.94 from current Liabilities to non-current Liabilities Section in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to:
Prepare a JEV to record as Receivables the disallowances which had become final and executory.
Maintain subsidiary ledger for each official/employee determined liable/responsible for the amount disallowed.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The Municipal Treasurer to issue Billing Statements for defaulting Market Stall Holders and furnish copies to the Municipal Accountant for recording, and monitor collections thereof.
The Municipal Accountant to record the receivables and income based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the LCE:
create an Inventory Committee that will be responsible in the conduct physical count on all Property, Plant and Equipment of the LGU,
render a complete and valid report thereon duly classified by type of PPE and,
reconcile the same with the accounting records, to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE accounts shown in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its Chairman and other officials to strictly follow the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR especially on the provisions of Liquidated Damages in the Contracts, and on the documentary requirements. We likewise recommend to submit the lacking documents, or else a Notice of Suspension shall be issued to demand submission.

We recommend that the LCE:
Advise the Municipal DRRM Council to consider only those PPAs which directly support Disaster Risk Reduction Management in allocating the DRRM Funds.
Require the MDRRMO to submit proposal for the annual operational requirement in the operation of the MDRRM Office for funding in the General Fund instead of the DRRM Fund.
Require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan in a manner that will categorically present the four Thematic Areas of the DRRM.

We recommended that management prepare and submit to the auditor the AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the audit report pursuant to the provision of Section 99 of the General Appropriation Act of 2019 to ensure that all recommendations are promptly acted upon and to provide enough time for the auditor to validate the same.

We recommended that the LCE:
Strictly enforce Municipal Ordinance No. 30-S-2003 which prohibits ambulant peddling of meat and fish products in the poblacion streets. This will add viability of the Market operation and at the same time maintain sanitation in the residential areas.
Require the BPLO to have the applications/contracts for Market Stall signed by the LCE and duly notarized for it to become enforceable and the monthly/quarterly rental rate be specifically stated.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to issue Billing Statement to stall holders with unpaid monthly rental fees.

We recommended that the LCE require persons liable concerned to settle immediately the disallowances pertaining to them, otherwise, require the Accountant to withhold any amount due them until the disallowance is fully settled.

We recommended that LCE require the Accountant and Treasurer to return to the Source Agencies, the unutilized funds from completed projects in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 to enable the source agencies to re-allocate the funds for other priority PPAs.

We recommended that LCE require:
Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion of the on-going PPAs for the benefits of the targeted constituents; and the MPDO to closely monitor the proper and timely implementation of the PPAs.
The MSWDO and Mun. Agriculturist to initiate the implementation of those PPAs which have not been implemented;
All Heads of Offices concerned to secure copies of the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the SA in order to have an appropriate guide in the implementation and monitoring of the post-implementation of the PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials to:
Implement immediately the unimplemented PPAs under the 20% DF for CY 2019 and prior years;
Henceforth implement within the timeline or as scheduled in the AIP thru an  updated preparation of technical documents for the projects on the part of the Municipal Engineer’s Office, and effetive monitoring on the part of the MPDC, consistent with  the mandated responsibility of LGU officials, as embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
Realign to other priority PPAs the unutilized funds from the original PPAs which are no longer feasible as determined by management.

We recommend that the LCE require the MDRRMO to initiate the procurement of the needed DRRM – equipage through the Bids and Awards Committee in order to enhance the LGU’s preparedness program. Likewise, we recommend that the stockpiling of goods for the constituents be implemented especially during the months where calamities often occur.

We recommend that the LCE require the newly appointed GAD focal person to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of programs and projects addressing issues on women’s empowerment and gender equality, targeting optimum number of results/outputs so that the maximum number of intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that LCE advise the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all the Revenue Collectors and the Liquidating Officer to turn-over daily and intact, all collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-382.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the Report of Collections submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned over to him, sign the certification and receipt portion and deposit intact all collections including the remittances of the Liquidating Officer.

We recommended that the LCE require the collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF, respectively, pursuant to Sec. 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382 in order to facilitate effective monitoring and control of the Accountable Forms of the LGU.

We recommended that: 

a) The management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 139.  The LGU’s Capability Agenda and prudent use of scarce financial resources should be observed in sending municipal officials and employees to conventions, seminars and similar activities.

b) The LCE require the three SB Members who did not attend the seminar to refund the registration fee paid to PLEASES, immediately.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation
Statement for each account maintained with the depository banks as
required under Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee to conduct the physical inventory, determine the actual
condition of these assets and render a report thereon pursuant to Section
156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual
on Property Custodianship.
Also, we recommend that all heads of departments prepare a list of
all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to
the Inventory Committee for validation and consolidation. Moreover,
require the Treasurer to update the property records.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any
discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary
adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an
agreement.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant and
Treasurer to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents as well as the original copies of all cancelled checks.
Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with the pertinent
provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of the RRSA.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices
of Suspension/Disallowance as a result of post audit of transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the recipient
Barangay specify in the Memorandum of Agreement for all fund transfers
to barangays the project implementation timeframe and submission of
Liquidation Reports with supporting documents within the prescribed
period.

We recommend that management determine and address the causes
for the non-implementation of the projects and expedite its implementation.
If the project is no longer necessary or needed by the municipality,
recommend for the reversion of the allocated funds for subsequent
appropriation to other important and achievable projects.

We recommend that management submit the required lacking
Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to ascertain the
validity, propriety and legality on the procurement of relief goods,
communications and disaster response and rescue equipment.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents
pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Further, ensure the full
implementation of all projects within the budget year.

We recommend that management strictly observe the rules and
regulations over the handling of collections, deposits, accountable forms and
reporting thereof.

We recommend that management require the Philippine Councilor’s
League and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a
government official receipt (Accountable Form No. 51) for all collections
made so that proper financial accountability could be established. Further,
ensure that all future payment for PCL/other leagues conducted
50
trainings/seminars be receipted with a government official receipt which
is AF No. 51.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to discontinue the practice of deducting from the salaries of
municipal officials and employees any amount of contribution or
payment of obligations to private financial institution other than those
allowed under Section 52 of GAA 2016.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee to conduct the physical inventory, determine the actual condition of these assets and render a report thereon pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship. Also, we recommend that all heads of departments prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the Inventory Committee for validation and consolidation. Further, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to update the property records.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive committed to create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee who will be responsible in the conduct of inventory of all the properties of the municipality for CY 2017. He also instructed all heads of department to prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1) Comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances; 2) Require the accountable officers to liquidate the overdue cash advances. observations.managementAction: Management stopped the practice of granting additional cash advances for officers and employees. Likewise, the FS for CY 2016. The Local Chief Executive committed to issue demand letters to those personnel already separated from the service.
